epwshiftr 0.1.4.9001
  • Articles
    • Manage CMIP6 CVs and Data Request
  • Manual
  • News

Manage CMIP6 CVs and Data Request

Hongyuan Jia

Source: vignettes/articles/cmip6_dict.Rmd
cmip6_dict.Rmd

Controlled Vocabularies (CVs) and Data Request

All CMIP6 outputs are written in NetCDF files in conformance with the CMIP standards. The Controlled Vocabularies (CVs) and Data Request play a key role in ensuring uniformity in the description of data sets across all models.

The CMIP6 CVs gives a well-defined set of global attributes that are recorded in each CMIP6 model output, providing information necessary for interpreting the data. The data of CVs for use in CMIP6 is stored as JSON files in a GitHub Repo.

The CMIP6 Data Request defines all the quantities from CMIP6 simulations that should be archived. This includes both quantities of general interest needed from most of the CMIP6-endorsed model intercomparison projects (MIPs) and quantities that are more specialized and only of interest to a single endorsed MIP. The Data Request data is stored a Microsoft Excel file (CMIP6_MIP_tables.xlsx) in a Subversion repo.

For more information, please see:

  • CMIP6 Global Attributes, DRS, Filenames, Directory Structure, and CVs
  • CMIP6 Data Request

Introduce the EsgDict class

{epwshiftr} provides an EsgDict class to help you fetch, parse and store CMIP6 CVs and Data Request.

Create an EsgDict object

You can create a new CMIP6 EsgDict object using the esgdict() function.

It is an R6 object with reference semantics. All methods in an R6 object can be invoked using the $method_name() style.

dict <- esgdict(project = "CMIP6")
dict
#> ══ ESG Dictionary ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#> • Project: "CMIP6"
#> • Profile: "cmip6"
#> • Status: "empty"
#> • Built at: <NONE>
#> 
#> ── Vocabulary ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#> • Vocab Version: <Empty>
#> 
#> ── Request ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#> • Request Version: <Empty>

You can use $is_empty() to check if there are any contents in current EsgDict.

dict$is_empty()
#> [1] TRUE

Fetch data of CVs and Data Request

After created, you can use $build() to fetch all data of CMIP6 CVs and Data Request.

The GitHub RESTful APIs are used to to fetch the latest tag of CVs and Data Request. You can specify your GitHub token by specifying the token argument in $build(). By default, it will uses GITHUB_PAT or GITHUB_TOKEN environment variable if exists.

Currently, all supported CVs are:

  1. drs: Data Reference Syntax (DRS).
  2. activity_id: Activity identifiers, e.g. HighResMIP, ScenarioMIP.
  3. experiment_id: Root experiment identifiers, e.g. historical, ssp126.
  4. frequency: Sampling frequencies, e.g. mon, day.
  5. grid_label: Grid identifiers, e.g. gn, gr.
  6. institution_id: Institution identifiers, e.g. AWI, CAS.
  7. nominal_resolution: Approximate horizontal resolutions, e.g. 50 km, 100 km.
  8. realm: Realms where variables are defined, e.g. atmos, ocean.
  9. required_global_attributes: Names of required global attributes.
  10. source_id: Model identifiers, e.g. GFDL-CM2-1, ACCESS-CM2.
  11. source_type: Model configurations, e.g. AGCM, OGCM.
  12. sub_experiment_id: Description of sub-experiment, e.g. none, s1960.
  13. table_id: Table identifiers, e.g. Amon, Oday.
dict$build()
#> Creating epwshiftr store directory '/home/runner/.local/share/R/epwshiftr'
#> ⠙ Fetching CMIP6 ESG Dictionary...
#> ⠙ Downloading data of CMIP6 CVs []...
#> ⠹ Downloading data of CMIP6 CVs [CMIP6_activity_id.json]...
#> ✔ Downloaded data of CMIP6 CVs successfully. [1.7s]
#> 
#> ⠙ Fetching CMIP6 ESG Dictionary...⠙ Downloading data of CMIP6 DReq...
#> ✔ Downloaded data of CMIP6 DReq successfully. [604ms]
#> 
#> ⠙ Fetching CMIP6 ESG Dictionary...✔ Fetched CMIP6 ESG Dictionary successfully at 2026-06-24 13:45:02.170551 [2.6s]
#> 
#> ══ ESG Dictionary ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#> • Project: "CMIP6"
#> • Profile: "cmip6"
#> • Status: "built"
#> • Built at: 2026-06-24 13:45:02 UTC
#> 
#> ── Vocabulary ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#> • Vocab Version: `6.2.60.0`
#> • Vocab Contents [13 types]:
#>   • drs [6 items]
#>   • activity_id [24 items]
#>   • experiment_id [322 items]
#>   • frequency [16 items]
#>   • grid_label [45 items]
#>   • institution_id [49 items]
#>   • nominal_resolution [15 items]
#>   • realm [8 items]
#>   • required_global_attributes [30 items]
#>   • source_id [132 items]
#>   • source_type [10 items]
#>   • sub_experiment_id [74 items]
#>   • table_id [43 items]
#> 
#> ── Request ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#> • Request Version: `1.0.33`
#> • Request Contents: 2062 Variables from 43 Tables and 9 Realms
dict$is_empty()
#> [1] FALSE

You can get the version of the CV collection and Data Request using $version() method. It returns a list of two numeric_versions giving you the current version of fetched CV collection (cvs) and Data Request (dreq):

dict$version()
#> $vocab
#> [1] '6.2.60.0'
#> 
#> $request
#> [1] '1.0.33'

The last built time can be retrieved using the $built_time() method:

last_built <- dict$built_time()
last_built
#> [1] "2026-06-24 13:45:02 UTC"

The last modified time of the CV collection and each CV can be retrieved using the $timestamp() method:

dict$timestamp()
#> $vocab
#> [1] "2025-09-04 23:01:22 UTC"
#> 
#> $drs
#> [1] "2025-09-02 22:42:23 UTC"
#> 
#> $activity_id
#> [1] "2018-03-06 00:39:09 UTC"
#> 
#> $experiment_id
#> [1] "2020-12-15 20:25:59 UTC"
#> 
#> $frequency
#> [1] "2021-05-24 14:48:15 UTC"
#> 
#> $grid_label
#> [1] "2017-09-09 01:12:00 UTC"
#> 
#> $institution_id
#> [1] "2022-08-16 04:41:00 UTC"
#> 
#> $nominal_resolution
#> [1] "2016-11-15 23:04:00 UTC"
#> 
#> $realm
#> [1] "2017-04-18 19:03:00 UTC"
#> 
#> $required_global_attributes
#> [1] "2019-12-19 23:32:17 UTC"
#> 
#> $source_id
#> [1] "2025-04-18 23:28:35 UTC"
#> 
#> $source_type
#> [1] "2017-09-09 00:57:00 UTC"
#> 
#> $sub_experiment_id
#> [1] "2019-06-17 18:01:51 UTC"
#> 
#> $table_id
#> [1] "2017-01-13 16:27:00 UTC"

Extract CV and Data Request data

The CV and Data Request data can be extracted using the $get(type) method, where type is the data type of interest. For Data Request, you can use "dreq" as the type name.

The returned data type are:

  1. For type "drs", “activity_id”,“frequency”,“grid_label”,“institution_id”,“source_type”and“sub_experiment_id”`, a [list].

  2. For type "experiment_id", "source_id" and "dreq", a [data.table].

  3. For "nominal_resolution", "required_global_attributes" and "table_id", a [character] vector.

# Data Request
dict$get("dreq")
#>       variable table_id modeling_realm                     standard_name
#>         <char>   <char>         <char>                            <char>
#>    1:      clt      3hr          atmos               cloud_area_fraction
#>    2:     hfls      3hr          atmos   surface_upward_latent_heat_flux
#>    3:     hfss      3hr          atmos surface_upward_sensible_heat_flux
#>    4:     huss      3hr          atmos                 specific_humidity
#>    5:     mrro      3hr           land                       runoff_flux
#>   ---                                                                   
#> 2058:     orog       fx           land                  surface_altitude
#> 2059:    rootd       fx           land                        root_depth
#> 2060:   sftgif       fx           land            land_ice_area_fraction
#> 2061:    sftlf       fx          atmos                land_area_fraction
#> 2062:    zfull       fx          atmos  height_above_reference_ellipsoid
#>                                                            long_name frequency
#>                                                               <char>    <char>
#>    1:                                   Total Cloud Cover Percentage       3hr
#>    2:                                Surface Upward Latent Heat Flux       3hr
#>    3:                              Surface Upward Sensible Heat Flux       3hr
#>    4:                                 Near-Surface Specific Humidity     3hrPt
#>    5:                                                   Total Runoff       3hr
#>   ---                                                                         
#> 2058:                                               Surface Altitude        fx
#> 2059:                                             Maximum Root Depth        fx
#> 2060:                                       Land Ice Area Percentage        fx
#> 2061: Percentage of the Grid Cell Occupied by Land (Including Lakes)        fx
#> 2062:                                  Altitude of Model Full-Levels        fx
#>            units                     cell_methods   cell_measures
#>           <char>                           <char>          <char>
#>    1:          %                 area: time: mean area: areacella
#>    2:      W m-2                 area: time: mean area: areacella
#>    3:      W m-2                 area: time: mean area: areacella
#>    4:          1           area: mean time: point area: areacella
#>    5: kg m-2 s-1 area: mean where land time: mean area: areacella
#>   ---                                                            
#> 2058:          m                       area: mean area: areacella
#> 2059:          m                       area: mean area: areacella
#> 2060:          %                       area: mean area: areacella
#> 2061:          %                       area: mean area: areacella
#> 2062:          m                       area: mean area: areacella
#>                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       comment
#>                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        <char>
#>    1:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           Total cloud area fraction (reported as a percentage) for the whole atmospheric column, as seen from the surface or the top of the atmosphere. Includes both large-scale and convective cloud.
#>    2:                                                                                                                                          The surface called 'surface' means the lower boundary of the atmosphere. 'Upward' indicates a vector component which is positive when directed upward (negative downward). The surface latent heat flux is the exchange of heat between the surface and the air on account of evaporation (including sublimation). In accordance with common usage in geophysical disciplines, 'flux' implies per unit area, called 'flux density' in physics.
#>    3:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              The surface sensible heat flux, also called turbulent heat flux, is the exchange of heat between the surface and the air by motion of air.
#>    4:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      Near-surface (usually, 2 meter) specific humidity.
#>    5:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      The total run-off (including drainage through the base of the soil model) per unit area leaving the land portion of the grid cell.
#>   ---                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        
#> 2058:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       The surface called 'surface' means the lower boundary of the atmosphere. Altitude is the (geometric) height above the geoid, which is the reference geopotential surface. The geoid is similar to mean sea level.
#> 2059:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      report the maximum soil depth reachable by plant roots (if defined in model), i.e., the maximum soil depth from which they can extract moisture; report as *missing* where the land fraction is 0.
#> 2060:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    Percentage of grid cell covered by land ice (ice sheet, ice shelf, ice cap, glacier)
#> 2061:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         Percentage of horizontal area occupied by land.
#> 2062: Height of full model levels above a reference ellipsoid. A reference ellipsoid is a mathematical figure that approximates the geoid. The geoid is a surface of constant geopotential with which mean sea level would coincide if the ocean were at rest. The ellipsoid is an approximation because the geoid is an irregular shape. A number of reference ellipsoids are defined for use in the field of geodesy. To specify which reference ellipsoid is being used, a grid_mapping variable should be attached to the data variable as described in Chapter 5.6 of the CF Convention.
#>                              dimensions out_name   type positive valid_min
#>                                  <char>   <char> <char>   <char>    <char>
#>    1:           longitude latitude time      clt   real     <NA>      <NA>
#>    2:           longitude latitude time     hfls   real       up      <NA>
#>    3:           longitude latitude time     hfss   real       up      <NA>
#>    4: longitude latitude time1 height2m     huss   real     <NA>      <NA>
#>    5:           longitude latitude time     mrro   real     <NA>      <NA>
#>   ---                                                                     
#> 2058:                longitude latitude     orog   real     <NA>      <NA>
#> 2059:                longitude latitude    rootd   real     <NA>      <NA>
#> 2060:                longitude latitude   sftgif   real     <NA>      <NA>
#> 2061:                longitude latitude    sftlf   real     <NA>      <NA>
#> 2062:         longitude latitude alevel    zfull   real     <NA>      <NA>
#>       valid_max ok_min_mean_abs ok_max_mean_abs
#>          <char>          <char>          <char>
#>    1:      <NA>            <NA>            <NA>
#>    2:      <NA>            <NA>            <NA>
#>    3:      <NA>            <NA>            <NA>
#>    4:      <NA>            <NA>            <NA>
#>    5:      <NA>            <NA>            <NA>
#>   ---                                          
#> 2058:      <NA>            <NA>            <NA>
#> 2059:      <NA>            <NA>            <NA>
#> 2060:      <NA>            <NA>            <NA>
#> 2061:      <NA>            <NA>            <NA>
#> 2062:      <NA>            <NA>            <NA>

# DRS
dict$get("drs")
#> $directory_path_example
#> [1] "CMIP6/CMIP/MOHC/HadGEM3-GC31-MM/historical/r1i1p1f3/Amon/tas/gn/v20191207/"
#> 
#> $directory_path_sub_experiment_example
#> [1] "CMIP6/DCPP/MOHC/HadGEM3-GC31-MM/dcppA-hindcast/s1960-r1i1p1f2/Amon/tas/gn/v20200417/"
#> 
#> $directory_path_template
#> [1] "<mip_era><activity_id><institution_id><source_id><experiment_id><member_id><table_id><variable_id><grid_label><version>"
#> 
#> $filename_example
#> [1] "tas_Amon_HadGEM3-GC31-MM_historical_r1i1p1f3_gn_185001-186912.nc"
#> 
#> $filename_sub_experiment_example
#> [1] "tas_Amon_HadGEM3-GC31-MM_dcppA-hindcast_s1960-r1i1p1f2_gn_196011-196012.nc"
#> 
#> $filename_template
#> [1] "<variable_id><table_id><source_id><experiment_id><member_id><grid_label>"
#> 
#> attr(,"version")
#> attr(,"version")$CV_collection_version
#> [1] '6.2.60.0'
#> 
#> attr(,"version")$CV_collection_modified
#> [1] "2025-09-04 23:01:22 UTC"
#> 
#> attr(,"version")$CV_modified
#> [1] "2025-09-02 22:42:23 UTC"
#> 
#> attr(,"version")$CV_note
#> [1] "Revised CMIP6_DRS directory and filename templates for CMOR 3.12"
#> 
#> attr(,"class")
#> [1] "Cmip6CV_DRS" "Cmip6CV"     "list"

# activity_id
dict$get("activity_id")
#> $AerChemMIP
#> [1] "Aerosols and Chemistry Model Intercomparison Project"
#> 
#> $C4MIP
#> [1] "Coupled Climate Carbon Cycle Model Intercomparison Project"
#> 
#> $CDRMIP
#> [1] "Carbon Dioxide Removal Model Intercomparison Project"
#> 
#> $CFMIP
#> [1] "Cloud Feedback Model Intercomparison Project"
#> 
#> $CMIP
#> [1] "CMIP DECK: 1pctCO2, abrupt4xCO2, amip, esm-piControl, esm-historical, historical, and piControl experiments"
#> 
#> $CORDEX
#> [1] "Coordinated Regional Climate Downscaling Experiment"
#> 
#> $DAMIP
#> [1] "Detection and Attribution Model Intercomparison Project"
#> 
#> $DCPP
#> [1] "Decadal Climate Prediction Project"
#> 
#> $DynVarMIP
#> [1] "Dynamics and Variability Model Intercomparison Project"
#> 
#> $FAFMIP
#> [1] "Flux-Anomaly-Forced Model Intercomparison Project"
#> 
#> $GMMIP
#> [1] "Global Monsoons Model Intercomparison Project"
#> 
#> $GeoMIP
#> [1] "Geoengineering Model Intercomparison Project"
#> 
#> $HighResMIP
#> [1] "High-Resolution Model Intercomparison Project"
#> 
#> $ISMIP6
#> [1] "Ice Sheet Model Intercomparison Project for CMIP6"
#> 
#> $LS3MIP
#> [1] "Land Surface, Snow and Soil Moisture"
#> 
#> $LUMIP
#> [1] "Land-Use Model Intercomparison Project"
#> 
#> $OMIP
#> [1] "Ocean Model Intercomparison Project"
#> 
#> $PAMIP
#> [1] "Polar Amplification Model Intercomparison Project"
#> 
#> $PMIP
#> [1] "Palaeoclimate Modelling Intercomparison Project"
#> 
#> $RFMIP
#> [1] "Radiative Forcing Model Intercomparison Project"
#> 
#> $SIMIP
#> [1] "Sea Ice Model Intercomparison Project"
#> 
#> $ScenarioMIP
#> [1] "Scenario Model Intercomparison Project"
#> 
#> $VIACSAB
#> [1] "Vulnerability, Impacts, Adaptation and Climate Services Advisory Board"
#> 
#> $VolMIP
#> [1] "Volcanic Forcings Model Intercomparison Project"
#> 
#> attr(,"version")
#> attr(,"version")$CV_collection_version
#> [1] '6.2.60.0'
#> 
#> attr(,"version")$CV_collection_modified
#> [1] "2025-09-04 23:01:22 UTC"
#> 
#> attr(,"version")$CV_modified
#> [1] "2018-03-06 00:39:09 UTC"
#> 
#> attr(,"version")$CV_note
#> [1] "Update activity_id to include CDRMIP and PAMIP"
#> 
#> attr(,"class")
#> [1] "Cmip6CV_ActivityId" "Cmip6CV"            "list"

# experiment_id
dict$get("experiment_id")
#>            experiment_id
#>                   <char>
#>   1:             1pctCO2
#>   2:       1pctCO2-4xext
#>   3:         1pctCO2-bgc
#>   4:         1pctCO2-cdr
#>   5:         1pctCO2-rad
#>  ---                    
#> 318:  volc-pinatubo-full
#> 319:  volc-pinatubo-slab
#> 320: volc-pinatubo-strat
#> 321:  volc-pinatubo-surf
#> 322:           yr2010CO2
#>                                                                                   experiment
#>                                                                                       <char>
#>   1:                                                      1 percent per year increase in CO2
#>   2:                                           extension from year 140 of 1pctCO2 with 4xCO2
#>   3:        biogeochemically-coupled version of 1 percent per year increasing CO2 experiment
#>   4:                                           1 percent per year decrease in CO2 from 4xCO2
#>   5:             radiatively-coupled version of 1 percent per year increasing CO2 experiment
#>  ---                                                                                        
#> 318:                                                                     Pinatubo experiment
#> 319:                                                     Pinatubo experiment with slab ocean
#> 320: Pinatubo experiment with partial radiative forcing, includes only stratospheric warming
#> 321:     Pinatubo experiment with partial radiative forcing, solar radiation scattering only
#> 322:                                                 concentration-driven fixed 2010 forcing
#>                                                                                                                                                                                                                            description
#>                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 <char>
#>   1:                                                                                                                                                                                                                     DECK: 1pctCO2
#>   2:                                                                                                                                   branched from 1pctCO2 run at year 140 and run with CO2 fixed at 4x pre-industrial concentration
#>   3:                                                                                                     Biogeochemically-coupled specified concentration simulation in which CO2 increases at a rate of 1% per year until quadrupling
#>   4:                                            1 percent per year decrease in CO2 (immediately after reaching 4xCO2 in the 1pctCO2 simulation); then held constant at pre-industrial level (part of the CDR-reversibility experiment)
#>   5:                                                                                                          Radiatively-coupled specified concentration simulation in which CO2 increases at a rate of 1% per year until quadrupling
#>  ---                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
#> 318: 1991 Pinatubo forcing as used in the CMIP6 historical simulations. Requires special diagnostics of radiative and latent heating rates. A large number of ensemble members is required to address internal atmospheric variability
#> 319:                                                                                                                                                                                      As volc-pinatubo-full, but with a slab ocean
#> 320:                                                                                                                              As volc-pinatubo-full, but with prescribed perturbation to the total (LW+SW) radiative heating rates
#> 321:                                                                                    As volc-pinatubo-full, but with prescribed perturbation to the shortwave flux to mimic the attenuation of solar radiation by volcanic aerosols
#> 322:                         Branch from beginning of year 2010 of the historical simulation with CO2 concentration and all other forcing held fixed at 2010 level (part of the CDR-yr2010-pulse experiment to diagnose CO2 emissions)
#>       tier start_year end_year min_number_yrs_per_sim required_model_components
#>      <int>      <int>    <int>                  <int>                    <list>
#>   1:     1         NA       NA                    150                     AOGCM
#>   2:     1         NA       NA                    210                     AOGCM
#>   3:     1         NA       NA                    150                 AOGCM,BGC
#>   4:     1         NA       NA                    200                 AOGCM,BGC
#>   5:     2         NA       NA                    150                 AOGCM,BGC
#>  ---                                                                           
#> 318:     1         NA       NA                      3                     AOGCM
#> 319:     3         NA       NA                      3                 AGCM,SLAB
#> 320:     1         NA       NA                      3                     AOGCM
#> 321:     1         NA       NA                      3                     AOGCM
#> 322:     3       2010     2115                    106                 AOGCM,BGC
#>      parent_experiment_id sub_experiment_id activity_id parent_activity_id
#>                    <list>            <list>      <list>             <list>
#>   1:            piControl              none        CMIP               CMIP
#>   2:              1pctCO2              none      ISMIP6               CMIP
#>   3:            piControl              none       C4MIP               CMIP
#>   4:              1pctCO2              none      CDRMIP               CMIP
#>   5:            piControl              none       C4MIP               CMIP
#>  ---                                                                      
#> 318:            piControl              none      VolMIP               CMIP
#> 319:         control-slab              none      VolMIP             VolMIP
#> 320:            piControl              none      VolMIP               CMIP
#> 321:            piControl              none      VolMIP               CMIP
#> 322:           historical              none      CDRMIP               CMIP
#>      additional_allowed_model_components
#>                                   <list>
#>   1:                        AER,CHEM,BGC
#>   2:                        AER,CHEM,BGC
#>   3:                            AER,CHEM
#>   4:                            AER,CHEM
#>   5:                            AER,CHEM
#>  ---                                    
#> 318:                        AER,CHEM,BGC
#> 319:                        AER,CHEM,BGC
#> 320:                        AER,CHEM,BGC
#> 321:                        AER,CHEM,BGC
#> 322:                            AER,CHEM

# frequency
dict$get("frequency")
#> $`1hr`
#> [1] "sampled hourly"
#> 
#> $`1hrCM`
#> [1] "monthly-mean diurnal cycle resolving each day into 1-hour means"
#> 
#> $`1hrPt`
#> [1] "sampled hourly, at specified time point within an hour"
#> 
#> $`3hr`
#> [1] "3 hourly mean samples"
#> 
#> $`3hrPt`
#> [1] "sampled 3 hourly, at specified time point within the time period"
#> 
#> $`6hr`
#> [1] "6 hourly mean samples"
#> 
#> $`6hrPt`
#> [1] "sampled 6 hourly, at specified time point within the time period"
#> 
#> $day
#> [1] "daily mean samples"
#> 
#> $dec
#> [1] "decadal mean samples"
#> 
#> $fx
#> [1] "fixed (time invariant) field"
#> 
#> $mon
#> [1] "monthly mean samples"
#> 
#> $monC
#> [1] "monthly climatology computed from monthly mean samples"
#> 
#> $monPt
#> [1] "sampled monthly, at specified time point within the time period"
#> 
#> $subhrPt
#> [1] "sampled sub-hourly, at specified time point within an hour"
#> 
#> $yr
#> [1] "annual mean samples"
#> 
#> $yrPt
#> [1] "sampled yearly, at specified time point within the time period"
#> 
#> attr(,"version")
#> attr(,"version")$CV_collection_version

# grid_label
dict$get("grid_label")
#> $gm
#> [1] "global mean data"
#> 
#> $gn
#> [1] "data reported on a model's native grid"
#> 
#> $gna
#> [1] "data reported on a native grid in the region of Antarctica"
#> 
#> $gng
#> [1] "data reported on a native grid in the region of Greenland"
#> 
#> $gnz
#> [1] "zonal mean data reported on a model's native latitude grid"
#> 
#> $gr

#>

#> [1] "regridded data reported on a grid other than the native grid and other than the preferred target grid"

dict$get("source_id")

#> 129: TaiESM1-TIMCOM2         2021                     NTU TaiESM1-TIMCOM2

#> 131:     UKESM1-1-LL         2021 MOHC,NERC,NIMS-KMA,NIWA     UKESM1.1-LL

#>   4:                                Australian Community Climate and Earth System Simulator Ocean Model Version 2
#>   5:                 Australian Community Climate and Earth System Simulator Ocean Model Version 2 quarter degree

#> 129:                                                   Taiwan Earth System Model 1.0 using TIMCOM ocean model 2.0

#> 131:                                                                                            UKESM1.1-N96ORCA1
#> 132:                                                                                          UKESM1.ice-N96ORCA1

#>   3:       <list[8]>    <list[6]>

# source_type
dict$get("source_type")
#> $AER
#> [1] "aerosol treatment in an atmospheric model where concentrations are calculated based on emissions, transformation, and removal processes (rather than being prescribed or omitted entirely)"
#> 
#> $AGCM
#> [1] "atmospheric general circulation model run with prescribed ocean surface conditions and usually a model of the land surface"
#> 
#> $AOGCM
#> [1] "coupled atmosphere-ocean global climate model, additionally including explicit representation of at least the land and sea ice"
#> 
#> $BGC
#> [1] "biogeochemistry model component that at the very least accounts for carbon reservoirs and fluxes in the atmosphere, terrestrial biosphere, and ocean"
#> 
#> $CHEM
#> [1] "chemistry treatment in an atmospheric model that calculates atmospheric oxidant concentrations (including at least ozone), rather than prescribing them"
#> 
#> $ISM
#> [1] "ice-sheet model that includes ice-flow"
#> 
#> $LAND
#> [1] "land model run uncoupled from the atmosphere"
#> 
#> $OGCM
#> [1] "ocean general circulation model run uncoupled from an AGCM but, usually including a sea-ice model"
#> 
#> $RAD
#> [1] "radiation component of an atmospheric model run 'offline'"
#> 
#> $SLAB
#> [1] "slab-ocean used with an AGCM in representing the atmosphere-ocean coupled system"
#> 
#> attr(,"version")
#> attr(,"version")$CV_collection_version
#> [1] '6.2.60.0'
#> 
#> attr(,"version")$CV_collection_modified
#> [1] "2025-09-04 23:01:22 UTC"
#> 
#> attr(,"version")$CV_modified
#> [1] "2017-09-09 00:57:00 UTC"
#> 
#> attr(,"version")$CV_note
#> [1] "Issue396 durack1 augment source_type with description (#399)"
#> 
#> attr(,"class")
#> [1] "Cmip6CV_SourceType" "Cmip6CV"            "list"

# sub_experiment_id
dict$get("sub_experiment_id")

# table_id
dict$get("table_id")
#>  [1] "3hr"         "6hrLev"      "6hrPlev"     "6hrPlevPt"   "AERday"     
#>  [6] "AERhr"       "AERmon"      "AERmonZ"     "Amon"        "CF3hr"      
#> [11] "CFday"       "CFmon"       "CFsubhr"     "E1hr"        "E1hrClimMon"
#> [16] "E3hr"        "E3hrPt"      "E6hrZ"       "Eday"        "EdayZ"      
#> [21] "Efx"         "Emon"        "EmonZ"       "Esubhr"      "Eyr"        
#> [26] "IfxAnt"      "IfxGre"      "ImonAnt"     "ImonGre"     "IyrAnt"     
#> [31] "IyrGre"      "LImon"       "Lmon"        "Oclim"       "Oday"       
#> [36] "Odec"        "Ofx"         "Omon"        "Oyr"         "SIday"      
#> [41] "SImon"       "day"         "fx"         
#> attr(,"version")
#> attr(,"version")$CV_collection_version
#> [1] '6.2.60.0'
#> 
#> attr(,"version")$CV_collection_modified
#> [1] "2025-09-04 23:01:22 UTC"
#> 
#> attr(,"version")$CV_modified
#> [1] "2017-01-13 16:27:00 UTC"
#> 
#> attr(,"version")$CV_note
#> [1] "Issue199 durack1 update table_id to Data Request v1.0 (#200)"
#> 
#> attr(,"class")
#> [1] "Cmip6CV_TableId" "Cmip6CV"         "character"

Store and reuse the EsgDict object

$save() method stores all the core data of current EsgDict object into a JSON file. It returns the full path of the saved JSON file.

dict_file <- file.path(tempdir(), "CMIP6DICT.json")
dict$save(dict_file)
#> [1] "/tmp/RtmpDDE2JL/CMIP6DICT.json"

This file can be reloaded via $load() method to restore the last state of current EsgDict object. It reads the JSON file and loads the CVs and Data Request data.

new_dict <- esgdict(project = "CMIP6")
new_dict$load(dict_file)
#> ✔ Loaded ESG Dictionary that was built at 2026-06-24 13:45:02 UTC.
#> ══ ESG Dictionary ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#> • Project: "CMIP6"
#> • Profile: "cmip6"
#> • Status: "loaded"
#> • Built at: 2026-06-24 13:45:02 UTC
#> 
#> ── Vocabulary ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#> • Vocab Version: `6.2.60.0`
#> • Vocab Contents [13 types]:
#>   • drs [6 items]
#>   • activity_id [24 items]
#>   • experiment_id [322 items]
#>   • frequency [16 items]
#>   • grid_label [45 items]
#>   • institution_id [49 items]
#>   • nominal_resolution [15 items]
#>   • realm [8 items]
#>   • required_global_attributes [30 items]
#>   • source_id [132 items]
#>   • source_type [10 items]
#>   • sub_experiment_id [74 items]
#>   • table_id [43 items]
#> 
#> ── Request ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#> • Request Version: `1.0.33`
#> • Request Contents: 2062 Variables from 43 Tables and 9 Realms
